---
title: "Trash as habitat for disease hosts"
---

# Trash as habitat for disease hosts

Investigating whether anthropogenic debris creates habitat for freshwater snail hosts and changes local disease ecology.

## Project angle

This project examines whether waste is not only a pollution problem, but also a potential ecological substrate that can shape host communities and disease-relevant habitat.

## Methods and outputs

- Field ecology
- Community-engaged research
- Snail-host habitat analysis
- Waste, water, and environmental health framing

## Publications / outputs

- Plastic pollution reshapes snail intermediate host life history: implications for schistosomiasis transmission. Yu, Mitchell, Vannatta, De Leo. 2026.
